claim: IQ4_XS greedy-decode parity vs a pinned llama.cpp reference on a real Llama-3.2-1B-Instruct IQ4_XS GGUF. Camelid's IQ4_XS resident-GPU and deterministic-CPU forward paths reproduce the reference token-for-token on full-budget prompts and match a probe-verified prefix before flipping at knife-edge near-ties (the reference token is Camelid's immediate #2). No exact-row-smoke or full-support promotion is implied.

reference.build: cmake + Visual Studio 17 2022 BuildTools x64, Release, -DGGML_CUDA=OFF -DLLAMA_CURL=OFF (CPU-only reference)

protocol.prompt_token_parity: verified identical between llama.cpp /tokenize (add_special) and Camelid for all 4 prompts (leading BOS 128000 + body)

results.prompt: The first three prime numbers are
Water is made of hydrogen and

near_tie.note: The reference token is Camelid's immediate #2 in the reference distribution; the gap is a knife-edge near-tie. Camelid's continuation (' atoms') is a coherent, arguably better completion.
Both Camelid paths agree on token 1 (id 13) and differ from the reference (id 627) at the first post-seed position; another knife-edge near-tie.

summary.gpu_resident_vs_reference: 2 of 4 prompts full 24-token token-identical (capital-france, first-primes); 2 of 4 match a probe-verified prefix then flip at a knife-edge near-tie at token index 1.
summary.cpu_deterministic_vs_reference: 1 of 4 full 24-token token-identical (first-primes); the other 3 match a verified prefix then flip at near-ties (index 13 / 1 / 1).
summary.interpretation: IQ4_XS is a lossy 4.25-bpw format; greedy decode is expected to flip at near-ties where the top-2 logit gap is within f32 numerical noise across engines. The verified prefixes, the reference-token-as-#2 evidence, and the coherent continuations show the IQ4_XS forward pass is numerically faithful, not buggy.

scope: This receipt certifies greedy-decode parity of the IQ4_XS quant forward pass against pinned llama.cpp acd79d6 for this specific Llama-3.2-1B model. It does NOT assert bit-exact logits, exact-row smoke certification, bounded-context/perf guarantees, or full support.
